executionPlan: Read-only audit. Read package.json/tsconfig/next.config plus projectContext.relevantFiles, expanded only along concrete imports (lib/persona, lib/gestures, useRealtimeChat, data/index, tailwind.config). Cross-checked entrypoints with scripts/company-map query. Extracted env-var NAMES only from .env.local.example and process.env references; never opened .env.local. No files edited.
summary: portfolio is a Next.js 14.1.4 App Router site (TypeScript, npm, Node 22.x) — a single-page terminal-themed developer portfolio whose hero is an interactive 3D talking avatar driven by the OpenAI Realtime API over WebRTC. The only server surface is app/api/realtime-session/route.ts, which mints a short-lived ephemeral token using server-only OPENAI_API_KEY and never returns that secret to the client. No test suite exists in the repo.

criteria.criterion: Profile facts each carry concrete evidence (file:line or package.json field)
criteria.evidence: See auditFacts.profileFacts — each entry has an evidence field pointing to package.json field or repo-relative file:line

criteria.criterion: Module inventory covers every real top-level source area with template fields and status
criteria.evidence: auditFacts.moduleInventory has 7 entries: app router, app/api/realtime-session, components/site, components/avatar, lib, data, public assets

criteria.criterion: realtime-session route and .env.local usage described without reproducing secrets
criteria.evidence: auditFacts.securityBoundaries names only ENV VAR NAMES (OPENAI_API_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_AVATAR_URL); no secret value or .env.local content reproduced

criteria.criterion: Every fact evidence-backed; unverifiable items omitted or marked hypothesis
criteria.evidence: Config-file ambiguity (next.config.ts vs .mjs) marked as hypothesis with reason; all other facts cite source

criteria.criterion: No product or memory file modified (read-only)

assumptions: Active Next config is next.config.mjs (which defines the CSP/security headers); the sibling next.config.ts is an unused stub — Next.js 14.1.4 loads .js/.mjs config and does not natively load next.config.ts (TS config support landed in Next 15). Marked hypothesis in facts.
Package manager is npm inferred solely from package-lock.json being the only lockfile present (no yarn.lock/pnpm-lock.yaml/bun.lockb).
'No tests' means no *.test.* or *.spec.* files exist under the repo (excluding node_modules); there is no configured test script in package.json either.
decisionsProposed: Onboard portfolio as a Next.js 14 App Router single-page portfolio; the single security-sensitive boundary to protect in future changes is app/api/realtime-session/route.ts (server-only OPENAI_API_KEY -> ephemeral token).

Note for future editors: repo contains duplicate config files (next.config.mjs is active + next.config.ts stub; postcss.config.js + postcss.config.mjs). Consider consolidating to avoid confusion, but out of scope for this audit.
lessonsProposed: company-map query confirmed the app-router entrypoint set (layout/page/provider/robots/sitemap/opengraph-image + the api route) without reading the whole tree — good first step before opening files.

profileFacts.evidence: tsconfig.json:6 "strict": true; sources are .ts/.tsx; devDependencies typescript ^5 (package.json:40)
package.json:22 next "14.1.4"; app/ directory with app/layout.tsx, app/page.tsx
package.json scripts (8-14) has no test; find for *.test.*/*.spec.* returns nothing outside node_modules

profileFacts.value: App Router with RSC by default; interactive UI opts into client components
profileFacts.evidence: app/layout.tsx is a server component with metadata export; app/page.tsx:1 "use client"; app/provider.tsx:1, components/avatar/*, components/site/* use "use client"

profileFacts.value: next-themes, attribute=class, defaultTheme=dark, enableSystem; custom Tailwind 'terminal' palette (term.*)
profileFacts.evidence: app/layout.tsx:92-97 ThemeProvider props; app/provider.tsx wraps next-themes; tailwind.config.ts:32-44 term color tokens (bg #050807, green #4ade80, etc.)

profileFacts.value: TailwindCSS ^3.3, tailwind-merge, clsx (cn helper), tailwindcss-animate, autoprefixer/postcss
JetBrains Mono via next/font/google, self-hosted, exposed as --font-mono
Google Analytics gtag (GA id in layout, loaded via next/script afterInteractive)
OpenAI Realtime API (gpt-realtime-2, voice cedar) — NOT Anthropic
next.config.mjs is the active config (defines CSP + security headers); next.config.ts is an unused stub (Next 14 does not load .ts config). Also duplicate postcss.config.js/.mjs
profileFacts.evidence: next.config.mjs:54-58 headers(); next.config.ts:3-5 empty config object
profileFacts.reason: Next.js 14.1.4 native config loading is .js/.mjs only (TS config support is a Next 15 feature); not verified by running a build

moduleInventory.responsibility: Next.js App Router root: HTML shell, SEO/metadata + JSON-LD, theme + GA bootstrap, and route-level generated assets (robots, sitemap, OG image, favicon). Renders the single-page portfolio via app/page.tsx.
moduleInventory.entrypoints: app/layout.tsx (RootLayout), app/page.tsx (Home), app/provider.tsx (ThemeProvider), app/robots.ts, app/sitemap.ts, app/opengraph-image.tsx, app/icon.svg, app/globals.css
moduleInventory.buildTestLintCommands: npm run build; npm run lint; tests: none
moduleInventory.dependenciesExternalServices: next, next-themes, next/font/google (JetBrains Mono, self-hosted), Google Analytics (gtag script). No secrets used here.
moduleInventory.securityDataBoundaries: Server component sets global CSP/security headers via next.config.mjs; GA script whitelisted in CSP. app/page.tsx dynamically imports TalkingAvatar with ssr:false (browser-only WebRTC/Canvas).

moduleInventory.responsibility: Only server-side endpoint. GET mints a short-lived ephemeral client secret from OpenAI so the browser can open a direct WebRTC session, injecting the avatar persona instructions and the two tool definitions (navigate_to_section, play_gesture).
moduleInventory.setupDevCommands: Requires OPENAI_API_KEY in .env.local; npm run dev
moduleInventory.buildTestLintCommands: npm run build / npm run lint; tests: none
moduleInventory.dependenciesExternalServices: OpenAI POST https://api.openai.com/v1/realtime/client_secrets. Imports buildInstructions() from lib/persona and GESTURES from lib/gestures. ENV VAR NAME: OPENAI_API_KEY.
moduleInventory.securityDataBoundaries: Reads process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY server-side only; returns 500 if unset (route.ts:14-20). Returns only the OpenAI response (ephemeral key at data.value) to the client — the long-lived OPENAI_API_KEY is never sent to the browser. Comment route.ts:4-5 confirms intent.

moduleInventory.responsibility: Static portfolio content sections rendered under the avatar hero: nav, about (bento grid), projects, experience, contact/footer, plus a reusable terminal-window wrapper.
moduleInventory.entrypoints: Nav.tsx, About.tsx, Projects.tsx, Experience.tsx, Contact.tsx (Contact + SiteFooter), TermWindow.tsx (TermSection)
moduleInventory.buildTestLintCommands: npm run build / npm run lint; tests: none
moduleInventory.dependenciesExternalServices: React client components; content from data/ (e.g. Projects.tsx imports { projects } from @/data); framer-motion for animation; no external network services.
moduleInventory.securityDataBoundaries: External project links rendered with target=_blank rel=noopener noreferrer (Projects.tsx:11). No secrets.

moduleInventory.responsibility: The interactive 3D talking-avatar hero: renders the r3f Canvas + GLTF avatar, runs the OpenAI Realtime voice loop over WebRTC, drives lip-sync from model audio volume, and plays body-gesture animations triggered by the user or by model tool-calls.
moduleInventory.entrypoints: TalkingAvatar.tsx (default export, Canvas + UI), Avatar.tsx (Avatar, GLTF/animation), useRealtimeChat.ts (WebRTC voice hook), AskHints.tsx (typewriter prompt hints)
moduleInventory.setupDevCommands: npm run dev (mic permission + OPENAI_API_KEY needed for live session)
moduleInventory.buildTestLintCommands: npm run build / npm run lint; tests: none
moduleInventory.dependenciesExternalServices: @react-three/fiber, @react-three/drei (useGLTF), three; OpenAI Realtime API — useRealtimeChat fetches /api/realtime-session for the ephemeral key then POSTs SDP to https://api.openai.com/v1/realtime/calls. Uses navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia (microphone). Loads GLB assets from public/.
moduleInventory.securityDataBoundaries: Client never sees OPENAI_API_KEY — only the ephemeral key from the server route (useRealtimeChat.ts:109-118). Mic access gated by browser + Permissions-Policy 'microphone=(self)' (next.config.mjs:46). Client function-call handler only runs whitelisted local actions (navigate_to_section, play_gesture) — useRealtimeChat.ts:6-24,171-192.

moduleInventory.responsibility: Shared non-UI logic: avatar persona/knowledge and system-instruction assembly, the gesture registry, and the cn() Tailwind class-merge helper.
moduleInventory.entrypoints: persona.ts (owner, skills, experience, education, extraKnowledge, buildInstructions()), gestures.ts (GESTURES, GESTURE_URLS, gestureUrl), utils.ts (cn)
moduleInventory.dependenciesExternalServices: clsx, tailwind-merge (utils.ts). No network/secrets. persona.buildInstructions() consumed by the API route; gestures consumed by API route + Avatar.
moduleInventory.securityDataBoundaries: persona.ts contains only public owner bio/contact info (name, email, LinkedIn) — no secrets. Note: owner email/phone are hardcoded public contact details, appropriate for a portfolio.

moduleInventory.responsibility: Static content data for the portfolio site sections (nav items, bento grid items, projects list) plus animation JSON assets.
moduleInventory.dependenciesExternalServices: None; plain TS/JSON. Imported via @/data (e.g. Projects.tsx:1).
moduleInventory.securityDataBoundaries: Public static content only; no secrets.

moduleInventory.responsibility: Static served assets: the 3D avatar model, gesture animation clips, and portfolio SVG/PNG imagery and background.
moduleInventory.entrypoints: public/avatar.glb (default AVATAR_URL), public/animations/*.glb (9 clips: wave, run, walk, dance, silly_dance, zombie, defeated, dodge, climb), public/*.svg and public/proj-*.png, public/bg.png
moduleInventory.dependenciesExternalServices: Consumed by components/avatar (useGLTF loads /avatar.glb and /animations/<name>.glb via lib/gestures.GESTURE_URLS) and components/site (img src=/proj-*.png, /b1.svg, etc.).
moduleInventory.securityDataBoundaries: Public static files; CSP media-src/img-src/worker-src allow 'self' + blob (next.config.mjs:31-36). No secrets.

realtimeSessionRoute.whatItDoes: Server-only GET handler (runtime nodejs, dynamic force-dynamic). Reads the OpenAI API key from the environment and POSTs to https://api.openai.com/v1/realtime/client_secrets to mint a short-lived ephemeral client secret, embedding the avatar persona instructions (lib/persona.buildInstructions) and two tool definitions. Returns the OpenAI JSON (ephemeral key at data.value) to the browser.
realtimeSessionRoute.evidence: route.ts:14 reads process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY; route.ts:23-25 sends it only in the server->OpenAI Authorization header; route.ts:84-86 returns only the OpenAI response body (ephemeral key) to the caller. Client (useRealtimeChat.ts:116-118) uses data.value/client_secret.value, never the long-lived key.

clientPublicEnv.note: Optional public override for the avatar GLB URL; NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix means it is intentionally client-exposed and holds no secret.

envLocalUsage.note: Secrets are supplied via .env.local (gitignored). Only OPENAI_API_KEY is required, per the committed template .env.local.example (which lists OPENAI_API_KEY= with no value). .env.local content was NOT read or reproduced.
envLocalUsage.evidence: .gitignore ignores .env* ; .env.local.example declares the variable name OPENAI_API_KEY only; grep of app/components/lib shows the only server secret reference is process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY (route.ts:14)

transportHardening.note: next.config.mjs sets a strict CSP (connect-src allows self + https://api.openai.com/*.openai.com + Google Analytics; script-src adds wasm-unsafe-eval for three.js KTX2, unsafe-eval only in dev), X-Frame-Options DENY, HSTS, and Permissions-Policy limiting microphone to self and disabling camera/geolocation.
